questions.question: What is the 'Boom and Bust' cycle?

options.text: The economic cycle of the stock market
The cycle of selling when desperate, then stopping access to deliver work, causing an empty pipeline later

questions.explanation: This cycle is caused by treating sales as an emergency reaction rather than a daily habit. It leads to inconsistent revenue.

questions.question: Why is optimizing 100% for 'Deep Work' dangerous for a solo founder?

options.text: Sales requires 'Shallow Work' (quick replies/outreach), so 100% Deep Work means 0% Sales

questions.explanation: You must balance the Maker schedule (Deep Work) with the Manager/Sales schedule (Shallow Work). Ignoring the latter kills the pipeline.

questions.question: When is the best time to do your 90-Minute Sales Block?

options.text: First thing in the morning (e.g., 7:00 AM - 8:30 AM)

questions.explanation: Willpower is a depleting resource. Doing sales first ensures it gets done before the fires and fatigue of the day take over.

questions.question: What metric should you track daily instead of Revenue?
questions.explanation: Revenue is a lagging indicator. Inputs (emails sent, calls made) are leading indicators that you can control every single day.

questions.question: What is the 'No-Zero Days' rule?

options.text: Never having zero dollars in the bank
Even on your busiest day, do at least ONE sales action

questions.explanation: Consistency is more important than intensity. Doing one small thing keeps the habit alive and preventing the 'restart friction' tax.
